nodes.title: Prompt Note
widgets_values:
Negative_Prompt :
  - Enter the negative prompt. When using Lightx2v LORA, the 
   CFG is set to "1", so the negative prompt seems to be 
   disabled. However, it can be reflected by introducing a 
   node called "NAG".

Positive_Prompt :
  - From v2.0, the node for wildcards has been discontinued.

nodes.title: Video Note
widgets_values:
RIFE VFI :
  - RIFE VFI is a frame interpolation node. It automatically creates frames between the frames of the original 16-frame video to smooth it out. Set the value of “Multiplier”
   in the “Input” area,  to "2" doubles the frame rate to 32 fps, while setting it to "4" converts it to 64 fps. While multiples of 2 are considered good, this has not been 
   verified. Generally,  30 fps is considered good for animation, and 60 fps for live-action. While VRAM is cleaned before this node, this node still puts a strain on VRAM, 
   so please be aware of this.

Upscale :
  - Use this if you want to upscale a 480p video to 720p. This uses a model, but you can replace it with your preferred node. However, the quality will be lower than if you 
   don't upscale, so if you have an environment where you can generate 720p from the beginning, we recommend not using upscale, even if it takes time.

nodes.title: Models Note
widgets_values:
Model loader :
  - Depending on whether you use a safetensors model such as fp8 or a GGUF model, Depending on whether you use a safetensors model such as fp8 or
  a GGUF model, In the "Fast Groups Bypasser" node, enable the either node. Enabling both "0.Safetensors_Models" and "0.GGUF_Models" will result 
  in an error, so set the one you want to use to "yes" and the one you don't to "no" depending on the models you use. The model is placed in the 
  following directory:    (Safetensors_Models)   ComfyUI/models/diffusion_models         (GGUF_Models)  ComfyUI/models/unet

Model Patch Torch Settings :
 - Setting "enable_fp16_accumulation" to "true" seems to reduce VRAM usage and improve processing speed. It seems to be more effective when used 
  together with the sage attention node. I don't know the details, so if you don't need it, set "Fast Groups Bypasser" to "no".

sage_attention : 
  - Sage attention improves processing speed. It is a little difficult to install. If you don't have it installed, set "Fast Groups Bypasser" to 
  "no."

Lightx2v LORA :
  - This is required for official models and models such as "smooth mix i2v v2.0". If you are using a model that already has lightx2v lora 
   merged, please bypass this. Each LORA is quite different, so it can be hard to decide which one is best, but if you're unsure, we 
   recommend the following LORA in Civitai.　https://civitai.com/models/1585622?modelVersionId=2337890

SVI LORA : 
  - For more details, please check the GitHub page.    https://github.com/vita-epfl/Stable-Video-Infinity/tree/svi_wan22?tab=readme-ov-file 

ModelSamplingSD3 :
  - 5 to 8 is recommended, but honestly you may not notice much difference. I've been using "5" lately, but I'm not sure.

Load CLIP Vision :
  - This workflow uses the Clip Vision model. I think it's a remnant of WAN2.1. Download the model from the link below and place it in the 
   following directory.   models/clip_vision    https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Wan_2.1_ComfyUI_repackaged/tree/main/split_files/clip_vision

nodes.title: Load Image_1st

nodes.title: Sampler Note
widgets_values:
Subgraph node :
  - Subgraph is a package of a part of a node. It is useful for organizing the entire workflow. You can view the contents by clicking the top right of the Subgraph node.

Wan Advanced I2V (Ultimate) :
  - For more details, please check the GitHub page. Make sure "continue_frames_count" is set to "1". If you copy this node, it may become "0". The other items are too difficult 
   to understand. I use the creator's standard value. It appears that only the older version can be installed via ComfyUI Manager. Based on my experience, the newer version, updated on 
   January 27th, has less color distortion, so I recommend cloning it from the GitHub page if possible. The older version will still work, but in that case, the "SVI Motion Strength" 
   setting will be disabled.　　　　https://github.com/wallen0322/ComfyUI-Wan22FMLF

WanVideoNAG :
  - NAG is introduced to reflect the contents of the negative prompt. When using Lightx2v LORA, CFG is set to "1", which disables the negative prompt. However, NAG seems to 
   consume VRAM, so you may want to bypass it depending on your PC environment. It is not clear how effective it will be.

CFGZeroStar :
  - I understand that CFGZeroStar was installed in the WAN2.1 era to roughly stabilize video. Like NAG, I installed it because I felt it had some effect, so you can bypass or 
   delete it if you like.

nodes.title: Input Note
widgets_values:
Fast Groups Bypasser (rgthree) :
  - This node bypasses nodes by group.
   Enabling both "0.Safetensors_Models" and "0.GGUF_Models" will 
   cause an error, so set the one you want to use to "Yes"
   and the one you don't want to use to "No."
   Also, if you're not using ”1.Lightx2v Lora"or"1.Patch Torch 
   Settings" or "1.Sage Attention," set them to "No." Using Sage
   attention can improve speed by about 30%, but this is limited to 
   NVIDIA GPUs and similar devices.
   This workflow can be generated up to six times in a row.
   If generating twice, set "1st & 2nd Section" and "2nd Video" to 
   "Yes,"and set the others to "No." If generating six times and 
   combining the videos,
   set "1st to 6th Section" and "6th Video" to "Yes,"
   and set "2nd to 5th Video" to "No."
   Also, if using "Upscale," set only the  upscale with the same 
   number as the video section you're generating to "Yes."

Seed (rgthree):
  - Use this if you want to fix the seed value. You can generate up to
   six times, with each generation having its own unique seed value.
   Normally, you should click "Randomize Each Time" so that "-1" is 
   displayed. Generation will be performed randomly. In that case, the 
   seed value for each generation will be displayed at the bottom. If 
   you want to fix the seed, click on the seed value field or enter 
   the seed value directly.
   For example, you can fix the first and second generations, and then
   regenerate randomly from the third generation onwards.
   However, if you regenerate a section before the generation section 
   you want to fix, the final frame will change, so you cannot fix 
   subsequent sections. As a general rule, regenerate after the 
   section you want to fix.

1st～12th Second :
  - Specify the length of the video you want to generate. Starting 
   with v3.0, you can now change the length of up to six generated 
   sections. This allows for more flexible control over operation.
   The number of seconds is specified. The total number of frames is 
   automatically calculated based on the set number of frames per 
   second.
   Note that WAN2.2 is based on a standard of 5 seconds and 81 
   frames, so exceeding this may cause a phenomenon known as loopback
   or a decrease in image quality. Incidentally, my PC's specs limit
   me to 7 seconds, and processing speed drops significantly if 
   I exceed this limit.

Frame_Rate :
  - Specifies the number of frames per second.
   The WAN2.2 14B model is trained with 16 frames, so 16 frames is 
   recommended, but you can change it to any number you like. 
   24 frames, etc., can also be used.

RIFE_VFI_Multiplier :
  - At the end of generation, specify the multiplier for frame 
   interpolation at the RIFE_VFI node. Setting it to "2" will result 
   in two frames of the specified number, while "4" will result in 
   four times the number. Incidentally, if you specify 16 frames per 
   second, "2" will result in 32 fps, and "4" will result in 64 fps. 
   I generally set it to four times the number.

Total & split steps : 
  - Set the number of steps. "total" is the number of steps for 
  both High and Low, and "split" is the number of steps for High 
  only. When using Lightx2v LORA, 
   a total of 4 to 8 steps is generally recommended. Consider the 
  balance between generation time and quality.

Width & Height :
  - Set the video size. Generally, 480 x 832 (480p) or 720 x 1280 
  (720p) is used. Any number is fine, but a multiple of 16 is 
  recommended. I often use 720 x 1072.

Structual_Repulsion_Boost & SVI_Motion_Strength :
  - This is the setting for the SVI I2V node "Wan Advanced I2V 
  (Ultimate)." For details, see GitHub. Honestly, I don't really 
  understand it, so I set it to "1," but I made it configurable. 
  "Boost" may affect movement, but I've confirmed that increasing 
  the value can cause color shifts.

Power Lora Loader :
  - Set LORA. You can apply a different LORA to each generation. 
  Using LORA is very important and has a big impact on the 
  generation results.
   You also need to give it the right weight. This will require a 
  lot of trial and error. A tip for beginners: the ideal weight is 
  the smallest value that allows 
   LORA to achieve the desired movement. The higher the weight, the
  greater the impact on quality.
